title: Pin NumPy <2.0.0 before torch-npu installation for CANN 8.x compatibility

meta.refiner_reasoning: CANN 8.1.RC1 tbe/te packages reference np.float_ which was removed in NumPy 2.0+. Pinning numpy<2.0.0 before torch-npu installation prevents the import preflight AttributeError. Validated in GLM-OCR migration: 5 Phase 5 iterations, fix resolved on iteration 1 with FULL_PASS verification. No project code changes needed — purely a dependency version constraint.

problem_description: SGLang's default launch behavior attempts CUDA graph capture and server warmup, both of which fail on Ascend NPU: (1) CUDA graph capture is a CUDA-specific feature not available on NPU, causing the server to crash during startup; (2) server warmup exercises operator patterns that may not have NPU implementations, causing spurious failures. Without these flags, the SGLang server fails to start or returns errors during initial requests.
rough_fix_approach: When launching SGLang on NPU (backend=ascend), always append --disable-cuda-graph and --skip-server-warmup to the sglang serve command. Additionally, --trust-remote-code should be added when the model uses custom HuggingFace model code (common for OCR/VLM models). These flags can be auto-injected by the serving validation script during Phase 3/Phase 5.

_asset_contents.rule.yaml:
title: Pin NumPy <2.0.0 before torch-npu installation for CANN 8.x compatibility
pattern: numpy[\s><=!~]*2[\d.]*
replacement: numpy<2.0.0
file_patterns: ["requirements*.txt", "*.txt", "setup.cfg", "pyproject.toml"]
target_roles: ["code_adapter"]
target_phases: ["phase_4_rule_migration"]
trigger_fingerprint: config_error|sglang_npu_launch_flags_missing|ascend-npu|cuda-graph-disable|launch-flags|server-warmup-skip|sglang-serving|ea1aa51548b1

id: e2e-v3-404af5e4e564-exp-Pin NumPy <2.0.0 before torch-npu installation for CANN 8.x compatibility

title: Python import shadowing: use importlib.import_module() for NPU custom-op patching to avoid function/module name collision in __init__.py exports

meta.refiner_reasoning: When __init__.py exports `from .submodule import func`, writing `import pkg.submodule` resolves to func (not module). Fix: use importlib.import_module('pkg.submodule') for monkey-patching module-level attrs. Validated on Deepwave acoustic_npu.py apply_npu_patch().

classifier.reasoning: Pattern appears whenever __init__.py re-exports shadow submodules — general Python import behavior, not project-specific

problem_description: import deepwave.acoustic as _mod resolved to exported function acoustic() rather than module, breaking NPU custom-op patching because _mod._update_velocities_opt doesn't exist on a function object. Silent at import time, manifests as AttributeError at patch time.
rough_fix_approach: Replace import package.submodule as _mod with _mod = importlib.import_module('package.submodule'). Add import importlib at file top. This bypasses __init__.py namespace and always returns the module object.

title: MISSING_COMPILED_BACKEND guard: return None in get_backend_function() when compiled C/CUDA extensions are absent on NPU

meta.refiner_reasoning: Compiled C/CUDA extensions (.so) are absent on NPU. Guarding get_backend_function() with early return None allows Python fallback dispatch. Universal pattern for any project migrating from CUDA that dispatches between compiled and interpreted backend.

classifier.reasoning: Deterministic pattern: flag set → safe None return; flag absent → crash. Applies universally to compiled-extension projects on NPU

problem_description: Projects with compiled C/CUDA backend .so files crash on NPU because the shared library is missing or incompatible. get_backend_function() calls fail with SystemError/ImportError even though pure-Python or torch.npu fallback exists.
rough_fix_approach: Add early-return guard at top of get_backend_function(): if MISSING_COMPILED_BACKEND: return None. Callers already handle None returns by falling back to Python implementation. One-line change, no caller modification needed.

title: CANN TBE dependency pre-flight: install decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf before any CANN operator execution on Ascend NPU

meta.refiner_reasoning: CANN TBE/TE packages import decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf at runtime. Missing each causes cryptic ImportError requiring separate pip install + retry cycle. Pre-flight install eliminates 5+ unnecessary repair iterations. Affects ALL CANN 8.x projects.

classifier.reasoning: Hard dependency constraint for ALL CANN 8.x projects. Static dependency list, trivially verifiable — absolutely generalizable

problem_description: CANN 8.5.0 TBE/TE packages import decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf at runtime. When missing from venv, CANN TBE init fails with cryptic ImportError/AttributeError not clearly indicating which package is missing. Each missing dep requires separate pip install + retry, adding 5+ repair iterations.
rough_fix_approach: Add pre-flight dependency install in Phase 2: pip install decorator attrs psutil cffi protobuf. Companion checker: .venv/bin/python -c "[__import__(m) for m in ['decorator','attrs','scipy','psutil','cffi','protobuf']]". No project code changes needed.

title: torchvision operator NPU availability: gracefully degrade metrics (LPIPS→skip, PSNR/SSIM→skimage CPU) instead of crashing when torchvision::nms has no NPU kernel

meta.refiner_reasoning: torchvision operators like nms lack NPU kernels (platform limitation). Wrapping torchvision-dependent metrics in try/except RuntimeError with informative skip prevents false-negative migration failures. PSNR/SSIM fallback via skimage (CPU).

classifier.reasoning: torchvision ops lacking NPU kernels vary by version, but try/except RuntimeError pattern is universally applicable for missing kernel scenarios

problem_description: torchvision is primarily CUDA/CPU; operators like nms have no NPU kernel. Calling torchvision-dependent metrics (LPIPS) crashes with RuntimeError: 'torchvision::nms' not implemented for 'npu', making the migration appear failed even though core computation works on NPU.
rough_fix_approach: Wrap torchvision metric calls in try/except RuntimeError. Log clear skip message with operator name. Fall back to CPU-based alternatives (skimage for PSNR/SSIM) for critical metrics. Non-critical metrics can be skipped with detailed platform limitation note.

title: Pybind .so loading via absolute path: use importlib.util.spec_from_file_location() to avoid sys.path.insert() accumulation that loads wrong custom_ops_lib.so in multi-module projects

meta.refiner_reasoning: Multi-module custom-op pybind projects produce identically-named custom_ops_lib.so. sys.path.insert(0, ...) accumulation causes Python to resolve import custom_ops_lib to wrong module. Fix: spec_from_file_location by absolute path with unique module name.

classifier.reasoning: Applies to any multi-module pybind project with identically-named .so files. Fix is robust and generalizable via importlib standard library

problem_description: Custom-op projects with multiple pybind modules each produce custom_ops_lib.so. sys.path.insert(0, path) accumulates paths; Python resolves import custom_ops_lib to first match in sys.path (not necessarily the correct one). Causes AttributeError because wrong module's exported functions don't match expected interface.
rough_fix_approach: Replace sys.path.insert(0, op_dir) + import custom_ops_lib with spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(f'{op_name}', os.path.join(op_dir, 'custom_ops_lib.so')); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod). Each op gets unique module name, no sys.path needed. 5-line replacement per op.
